pricing_status_legend.public_pricing_available: A public page reviewed in this run displayed a numeric premium, from-price, or calculator output without requiring broker contact, login, or a completed quote form beyond basic page access.
pricing_status_legend.quote_flow_pricing_only: The public source exposes a quote or application flow and may show premiums after user inputs, but no generic numeric public price was visible in the reviewed source extract for this run.
pricing_status_legend.no_public_pricing_found: The reviewed public source(s) in this run did not expose numeric pricing; pricing appears quote-gated, broker-gated, blocked, or absent.

method_notes: This ledger records only what was publicly visible in reviewed sources during this discovery run.
Absence of public pricing here means no numeric public premium was found in the reviewed source extracts; it does not prove the insurer never shows prices deeper in an interactive quote flow.
Quote-form presence without a visible reviewed number was separated into quote_flow_pricing_only so later runs can prioritize deeper quote testing if needed.
pricing_availability_method_notes: The pricing_availability_status field normalizes discovery-time visibility into four buckets: public_pricing_available, quote_flow_gated, partially_visible, and unavailable_during_discovery.
partially_visible means the source exposed pricing-adjacent evidence such as discounts, premium mechanics, or price-result placeholders without a usable numeric premium.
quote_flow_gated means the reviewed source suggested pricing should appear after interactive quote inputs, but this run did not capture the numeric result.
pricing_transparency_method_notes: pricing_transparency_friction records the specific obstacle observed for each candidate during public pricing discovery, rather than pretending every no-price case failed in the same way.

Candidates with no directly usable public price are explicitly separated into blocked public pricing, incomplete quote output, eligibility-gated results, failed or abandoned quote retrieval, contact/account-gated pricing, broker-led public paths, and brochure-or-marketing-only public evidence.

A candidate can carry secondary frictions when multiple barriers were visible in reviewed evidence, but primary_friction captures the best single explanation for why usable public pricing was not obtained in this run.

pricing_friction_event_count counts the structured pricing-friction evidence entries added per primary and secondary friction, each with retrieval metadata, reviewed source URL/provider, access date, and an explicit obtainable/not-obtainable note.

pricing_availability_status_legend.public_pricing_available: A reviewed public source displayed a numeric premium, from-price, or calculator output during discovery without requiring a completed quote, broker contact, or login.
pricing_availability_status_legend.quote_flow_gated: Public evidence indicates pricing is available through a quote or application flow, but no numeric premium was visible in the reviewed discovery extract.
pricing_availability_status_legend.partially_visible: Public evidence exposed some pricing signal such as discounts, premium mechanics, rate-related UI, or price-result placeholders, but not a usable numeric premium for discovery.
pricing_availability_status_legend.unavailable_during_discovery: No usable pricing was visible in reviewed public evidence during discovery because pricing was absent, blocked, challenge-gated, brochure-only, or otherwise not observable.

pricing_transparency_friction_legend.public_numeric_pricing_visible: A usable numeric premium or from-price was visible in reviewed public evidence during discovery.
pricing_transparency_friction_legend.blocked_public_pricing: A public pricing or quote path existed but was blocked by a challenge wall, login wall, reCAPTCHA, or similar access barrier before usable pricing was visible.
pricing_transparency_friction_legend.incomplete_quote_output: The reviewed public source showed pricing-related UI, placeholders, price-result fields, or quote CTAs, but the run did not obtain a usable numeric premium from that path.
pricing_transparency_friction_legend.eligibility_gated_results: Pricing appears to depend on passing upfront eligibility or household-profile steps such as residency, duration, nationality, age, or dependent composition before any result is visible.
pricing_transparency_friction_legend.failed_or_abandoned_quote_retrieval: A quote path appeared to exist, but retrieval did not reach usable pricing because the observed path failed technically, redirected away, depended on an unverified successor flow, or was otherwise abandoned during discovery.
pricing_transparency_friction_legend.contact_or_account_gated_pricing: Pricing appears to require account creation, login, or contact submission before any usable quote result is shown.
pricing_transparency_friction_legend.broker_or_intermediary_only_public_path: The reviewed public path was broker-led or intermediary-led rather than a verified insurer-controlled pricing path, so direct public pricing transparency remained unresolved.
pricing_transparency_friction_legend.brochure_or_marketing_only_no_price: Reviewed public materials were brochure-led or marketing-led and did not expose a public pricing path with usable numeric premiums.

method_notes: pricing_friction_evidence_entries stores one source-backed ledger entry for each observed pricing-friction event, including retrieval_method, source_url, source_provider, access_date, and a short note describing what pricing was and was not obtainable during discovery.

Pricing-friction entries are discovery-time observations only; they do not infer hidden downstream quote results, non-public broker pricing, or underwriting outcomes that were not visible in reviewed public sources.
